description: At the door, 'pao' closes a visit or conversation when people part. It says goodbye without predicting how long the separation will last; 'kia' (hello) opens the corresponding encounter.
articulatory_notes: Close the lips for unvoiced 'p' and release them into open central 'a'. Keep the syllable break as the tongue draws back and the lips round for mid-back 'o'. Stress 'pa'.
usage_notes: Alone, 'pao' is enough; a name or form of address may accompany it. Voice and the surrounding moment carry the social tone of the farewell.
